meta.name: AHS Adult Hope Scale
meta.description: The AHS is a 12-item self-report scale (8 scored items, 4 fillers) that assesses an individual’s level of hope. Hope comprises two dimensions: Pathways thinking (finding ways to achieve goals) and Agency thinking (motivation to pursue goals). It is a core concept in positive psychology.
meta.author: Snyder, Harris, Anderson, Holleran, Irving, Sigmon, et al. (1991)
meta.reference: Snyder CR, Harris C, Anderson JR, et al. The will and the ways: development and validation of an individual-differences measure of hope. J Pers Soc Psychol. 1991;60(4):570-585.
meta.instruction: Read each item and select the option that best describes your current thinking.

questions.text: I can think of many ways to get out of a jam
I feel tired most of the time
There are lots of ways around any problem
I am easily downed in an argument
I can think of many ways to get the things in life that are most important to me
Even when others get discouraged, I know I can find a way to solve the problem
My past experiences have prepared me well for my future
I’ve been pretty successful in life
I usually find myself worrying about something
I meet the goals that I set for myself

ranges.label: Low Hope
ranges.description: Your hope level is low. You may lack confidence and pathways when facing difficulties.
suggestions: Try setting small, achievable goals to build confidence
Spend time with positive, optimistic people
Consider seeking counseling to enhance hopefulness

ranges.label: Below Average Hope
ranges.description: Your hope is below average. You may occasionally feel lacking in motivation or direction when pursuing goals.
suggestions: Review past successes to boost confidence
Explore multiple pathways to achieve your goals

ranges.label: Above Average Hope
ranges.description: Your hope is above average. You are generally good at setting goals and finding ways to achieve them.
suggestions: Use your hopefulness as motivation when facing challenges
Help others develop a sense of hope

ranges.label: High Hope
ranges.description: Your hope level is very high. You maintain a positive attitude and find multiple solutions when facing difficulties.
suggestions: Your high hope is a valuable psychological resource
Use your positive mindset to help and encourage others
